Build a bounded fetch plan

Source: R/fetch-public.R
gx_fetch_plan.Rd

Collapses a validated gx_catalog to one deterministic row per distribution, intersects requested time coverage, applies the built-in first-match handler registry, and records count and byte budgets. Planning performs no DNS lookup or provider request.

Usage

gx_fetch_plan(
  catalog,
  time = NULL,
  handlers = gx_handlers(),
  max_datasets = 100L,
  max_bytes = 1e+09
)

Arguments

catalog

A validated gx_catalog object.

time

NULL, two UTC POSIXct values, or an exact list containing start and end UTC bounds.

handlers

The exact built-in registry returned by gx_handlers().

max_datasets

Maximum number of distributions to select.

max_bytes

Maximum aggregate encoded bytes and maximum aggregate decoded bytes.

Value

A validated gx_fetch_plan object.

Details

The frozen M7 contract accepts only the built-in registry returned by gx_handlers(). Runtime registration is deferred. max_bytes is applied independently to encoded and decoded response budgets, and each selected distribution receives at most one physical-attempt reservation.

On this page

geoconnexr Experimental software; verify results before operational use.

Developed by Kyle Onda. Site built with pkgdown 2.2.1.
